text.title: ES Snapshots Failure Modes
text.summary: Three antipatterns observed when adopting snapshots. Symptom + root cause + fix per item.

antipattern.symptom: Loader reads snapshot and skips replay entirely. A bug in `_apply_OrderPlaced` produced wrong state in the snapshot. Production runs on the wrong state until snapshots are manually deleted.

root-cause.source: ES fundamentals — events as source of truth

antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped replay-from-zero on snapshot rot.
antipattern.fix: Always replay events with version > snapshot_version on top of the snapshot. Add a periodic CI job that diffs snapshot-loaded vs replay-from-zero state and alerts on divergence.

antipattern.symptom: Event class `OrderPlaced` renamed `customer_id` to `buyer_id`. Snapshots still encode old field. New `apply` handler reads `buyer_id` and gets `None`. Aggregate state corrupted at load.
antipattern.fix: CI gate: snapshot invalidation script MUST run before the new event version deploys. Lock the deploy until invalidation completes.

antipattern.symptom: Snapshot deserialization runs domain logic to "fix up" old shapes. Logic drifts; produces different state than replay. The "smart" snapshot diverges from the log.
antipattern.root-cause: Author added correction logic instead of relying on log replay.
antipattern.fix: Remove correction logic from snapshot deserializer. On version mismatch, fall back to replay-from-zero (which will write a fresh snapshot at next interval).
